cases.prompt: Run stage 1 /he:plan and say if /he:implement is ready.
Stage 1 is PASS. Run stage 2 /he:implement and say if /he:verify is ready.
Implementation is done. Run stage 3 /he:verify and say if /he:ship is ready.
Verification is clean and committed. Run stage 4 /he:ship and say if /he:learn is needed.
Ship found a repeated miss. Run stage 5 /he:learn and close the loop.
Refactor reusable dashboard cards to use design tokens instead of hardcoded colors and spacing.
Review this service boundary, public API shape, wrapper layer, and ownership split before we refactor.
Find callers, dependencies, routes, and impact before changing BookingStatus.
Create and verify an investor one-pager PDF from an HTML draft.
A flaky regression throws an unexplained exception. Diagnose it before fixing.
The checkout UI changed; run real browser regression proof with artifacts.
I have a product idea. Interview me one question at a time before planning.
The feature is committed. Validate it, push safely, open the PR, and follow CI.
The dashboard query has bad p95 latency. Measure it and make it faster.
Record and polish a product demo walkthrough video for launch.
We tried three fixes and hit the same issue again. Capture the durable lesson before final.
Do a defensive appsec review of auth, secrets, dependencies, and data exposure.
Reply to the user briefly while preserving the exact command, path, and error text.
Review these tests for behavior assertions, QA gaps, and targeted failure proof.
Review this PR harshly for maintainability, wrappers, weak tests, giant files, and abstractions.
Turn this accepted PRD into agent-ready vertical-slice issues.
Turn the resolved conversation and repo evidence into a concise PRD and implementation brief.
Use Treehouse to lease a reusable worktree, check status, and return it when done.
Which workflow, skill, or next step should I use from idea to PR?
